steps.title: Use unit circle coordinates to find sine and cosine
steps.content:
On a unit circle, a point at angle $\theta$ from the positive $x$-axis has coordinates $(\cos\theta, \sin\theta)$. Point $A = (1,0)$ is at angle $0$. Point $B = \left(\frac{5}{\sqrt{34}}, -\frac{3}{\sqrt{34}}\right)$ is on the unit circle. If angle $p$ is the angle from ray $OA$ to ray $OB$ (counterclockwise positive), then $B$ is located at angle $p$, so by the unit circle property:
$$\cos p = \frac{5}{\sqrt{34}} \quad \text{and} \quad \sin p = -\frac{3}{\sqrt{34}}$$
